title: Available Skills
description: A list of the skills included in the Algorand Agent Skills repository.
content:
# Available Skills

The following skills are available to supercharge your development workflow:

| Skill | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| `create-project` | Initialize AlgoKit projects with best practices. |
| `build-smart-contracts` | Build contracts in Algorand TypeScript (PuyaTs) or Python. |
| `test-smart-contracts` | Write and run contract tests. |
| `call-smart-contracts` | Deploy and interact with contracts. |
| `deploy-react-frontend` | Build React frontends for dApps. |
| `use-algokit-cli` | AlgoKit CLI command reference. |
| `troubleshoot-errors` | Diagnose and fix common Algorand errors. |

## x402 Protocol Skills

Skills for the x402 HTTP-native payment protocol:

* **`algorand-x402-typescript`**: Clients, servers, and facilitators in TS.
* **`algorand-x402-python`**: Clients, servers, and facilitators in Python.

## MCP Servers

Skills also provide access to Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers:

* **Kapa MCP**: Semantic search over official Algorand documentation.
* **GitHub MCP**: Access canonical examples from Algorand repositories.

title: Agent Skills Overview
description: Learn about Algorand Agent Skills and how they can supercharge your development.
content:
# Algorand Agent Skills

**Algorand Agent Skills** are specialized instructions and tools that enable AI coding assistants to understand and perform Algorand-specific development tasks.

By adding these skills to your AI (like Claude, Cursor, or Copilot), you transform it from a general-purpose assistant into an expert Algorand developer that knows:

* **Best Practices**: The right way to structure contracts and dApps.
* **Library Nuances**: Deep knowledge of AlgoKit Utils, PuyaTS, and more.
* **Common Pitfalls**: How to avoid and fix frequent errors.

## Why use Agent Skills?

1. **Speed**: Write complex smart contracts in minutes by describing them in plain English.
2. **Accuracy**: AI assistants loaded with skills are less likely to hallucinate incorrect API calls.
3. **Onboarding**: New developers can become productive immediately without spending weeks reading documentation.

## How they work

Each skill consists of:

* **Instructions**: Guidelines for the AI on how to perform a task.
* **Reference Material**: API documentation and code examples that the AI can "read" on demand.
* **MCP Servers**: Tools that allow the AI to search official docs or fetch live blockchain data.

Ready to get started? Head over to the [Quick Start](/docs/agent-skills/quick-start) guide.

title: Quick Start
description: Set up Algorand Agent Skills in your favorite AI coding tool.
content:
# Quick Start with Agent Skills

Follow these steps to set up Agent Skills in your development environment.

## 1. Prerequisites

You need an AI coding assistant. We recommend one of the following:

* **Claude Code**
* **Cursor**
* **GitHub Copilot**
* **OpenCode**

## 2. Setup (Per Project)

Choose your tool and follow the instructions:

### Local Setup (Recommended)

If you have the `algorand-agent-skills` repository cloned locally:

```bash
# In your project root
cp -r ../algorand-agent-skills/skills ./
cp ../algorand-agent-skills/setups/AGENTS.md ./
```

Follow the tool-specific guides below to integrate these skills:

### Cursor

1. Create a `.cursor/skills` folder in your project.
2. Copy the skill files: `cp -r ../algorand-agent-skills/skills/* .cursor/skills/`
3. Configure your `mcp.json` to include the Algorand MCP server.

### Claude Code

```bash
# Copy the skills to your project
cp -r ../algorand-agent-skills/skills ./
# Copy the agent configuration
cp ../algorand-agent-skills/setups/claude-code/.mcp.json ./
cp ../algorand-agent-skills/setups/claude-code/CLAUDE.md ./
```

### GitHub Copilot

1. Copy the `skills` folder to your project root.
2. Create a `.github/copilot-instructions.md` file and paste the instructions from the `setups/copilot` folder.

## 3. GitHub PAT Setup

Most skills require access to search code examples. You'll need a GitHub Personal Access Token:

1. Go to [GitHub Settings > Tokens](https://github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens).
2. Create a new token with read access to public repositories.
3. Set the `GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable in your terminal.

```bash
export GITHUB_TOKEN="your_token_here"
```

title: Contributing
description: How to contribute to the Algorand JNTU ecosystem.
content:
# Joining the Community

We welcome contributions from developers, instructors, and advocates! Whether it's adding a new agent skill or creating tutorial content, your input is valuable.

1. **Fork the Repo**: Start by forking the relevant project on GitHub.
2. **Create a Branch**: Work on your feature or fix in a dedicated branch.
3. **Submit a PR**: Provide a clear description of your changes.

Check out our [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/algorand-devrel) for any major proposals.

title: How to Start
description: A quick guide on how to start with Algorand development.
content:
# How to Start with Algorand

Starting your journey as an Algorand developer is easy. Follow these steps to set up your environment and make your first transaction.

## 1. Where to Start?

The best place to start is the **Algorand Developer Portal**. It contains comprehensive guides, API references, and tutorials.

* [Algorand Developer Portal](https://developer.algorand.org/)
* [AlgoKit Documentation](https://github.com/algorandfoundation/algokit-cli)

## 2. Setting Up Your Environment

You need a few things installed on your machine to get started:

* **Python**: Most Algorand tooling is built on Python.
* **Node.js**: For frontend development and some SDKs.
* **Docker**: To run a local private network (Algorand "LocalNet").

## 3. Install AlgoKit

AlgoKit is the primary tool for Algorand development. It helps you initialize projects, run local networks, and deploy contracts.

```bash
# Install via pip
pipx install algokit

# Verify installation
algokit --version
```

## 4. Spin up LocalNet

Once AlgoKit is installed, you can start a local Algorand node for testing:

```bash
algokit localnet start
```

## 5. Your First Project

Create a new project using an AlgoKit template:

```bash
algokit init
```

Follow the prompts to choose your preferred language (TypeScript or Python).

title: Introduction
description: Welcome to the Algorand JNTU Onboarding Documentation.
content:
# Welcome to Algorand JNTU

The **Algorand JNTU** program is designed to onboard developers into the Algorand ecosystem, providing the tools and knowledge needed to build scalable, secure, and decentralized applications on the world's most advanced blockchain.

## Getting Started

To begin your journey with Algorand, we recommend watching our introductory video and setting up your development environment.

### Video: Getting Started with Algorand

<iframe width="100%" height="450" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/HrZUe5P4mUY" title="Algorand Getting Started" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen className="rounded-xl border shadow-sm" />

> \[!NOTE]
> *Replace the video ID above with your actual YouTube video ID.*

## Algorand Connect Wallet Starter

To get your frontend up and running quickly, use the **Connect Wallet Starter V2**. This template provides the easiest way to integrate Algorand wallets into your React application.

### [Connect Wallet Starter V2](https://github.com/nickthelegend/connect-wallet-starter-v2)

**Setup Instructions:**

1. **Clone the project**:
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/nickthelegend/connect-wallet-starter-v2.git
   ```
2. **Install dependencies**:
   ```bash
   cd connect-wallet-starter-v2
   npm install
   ```
3. **Run locally**:
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```

***

## Onboarding Users without Wallets (Custodian)

One of the biggest hurdles in blockchain is user onboarding. **Kyra Wallet** (the custodian) allows users to login using **Google**—no need for Pera or any other external wallet to get started.

### [Kyra Wallet Custodian Starter](https://github.com/nickthelegend/connect-wallet-starter-v2-custodian)

**Why use the Custodian Wallet?**

* **No Crypto Experience Needed**: Users can sign in with social accounts they already have.
* **Seamless Integration**: The wallet is generated automatically in [kyra.algocraft.fun](https://kyra.algocraft.fun).
* **Developer Friendly**: Use the starter template to build apps that onboard users instantly.

***

## Algorand Agent Skills

**Algorand Agent Skills** are a power pill for development. They teach AI assistants (like Claude, Cursor, or Copilot) how to complete Algorand-specific tasks.

* **Create Projects**: Initialize AlgoKit projects with best practices.
* **Build Smart Contracts**: Build contracts in Algorand TypeScript (PuyaTs) or Python.
* **Test & Deploy**: Automated workflows for testing and deploying your dApps.
* **Troubleshoot**: Diagnose and fix common Algorand errors instantly.

## Next Steps

Head over to the [How to Start](/docs/getting-started/how-to-start) guide or explore the [Agent Skills](/docs/agent-skills) documentation.

title: Starters & Templates
description: Quick-start templates for Algorand frontend and custodian integration.
content:
# Starters & Templates

Jumpstart your development with these pre-configured templates.

## 1. Connect Wallet Starter V2

The easiest way to integrate Algorand wallets into your React application. Supports Pera, Defly, and other major wallets out of the box.

* **GitHub**: [connect-wallet-starter-v2](https://github.com/nickthelegend/connect-wallet-starter-v2)
* **Features**:
  * Wallet Connection UI
  * Transaction Signing examples
  * Account balance fetching

## 2. Custodian Wallet Starter

Specifically designed for onboarding users who don't have a blockchain wallet yet. Allows users to login via **Google**.

* **GitHub**: [connect-wallet-starter-v2-custodian](https://github.com/nickthelegend/connect-wallet-starter-v2-custodian)
* **Live Demo**: [kyra.algocraft.fun](https://kyra.algocraft.fun)
* **Key Benefit**: Zero-friction onboarding for non-crypto users.

## 3. Deployment Guide

Most starters can be deployed instantly to **Vercel** or **Netlify**:

1. Push your code to GitHub.
2. Connect your repo to Vercel/Netlify.
3. Add any required environment variables (like `NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOD_SERVER`).
4. Build and Deploy!
